rationale_overall: Quiet rebalance at cap=5: every one of the five held theses got reinforced inside the Q3 2025 lookback, and no single name is weak enough to swap out without a stronger replacement. NVDA's Q2 FY26 print on Aug 27, 2025 ($46.7B revenue +56% YoY, Data Center $41.1B +56% YoY with Blackwell up 17% sequentially) confirms the AI surge is still compounding. RKLB's Aug 7, 2025 Q2 print delivered record $144M revenue (+36% YoY) with $1.067B backlog; Neutron timing slipped but the small-launch and space-systems franchise is intact. IONQ's Oxford Ionics acquisition closed Sep 17, 2025 (a $1.075B deal consummated, not just announced), locking in the quantum buildup-phase signal the profile names. LNG continues to ride record European exports on ~85% take-or-pay 20-year contracts. URA's nuclear-AI thesis is reinforced by the Meta-Constellation 20-year PPA at Clinton (announced June 3, 2025) following Microsoft's Three Mile Island restart deal, with hyperscaler PPAs now an established cadence. No robotics or demographics name surfaced this quarter with a concrete commercial milestone strong enough to displace one of the five held theses at cap=5, so no churn is the right call. Wave coverage unchanged: AI (NVDA), nuclear (URA), rockets/spacecraft (RKLB), geopolitical-energy (LNG), quantum (IONQ) - five distinct buckets, one ticker each, all in buildup or surge per the profile's thesis.
